title: What a Memory Café taught me about listening
excerpt: Every Tuesday afternoon, I sit in a room full of people whose names I now know by heart. What started as a programme became a reminder of what it means to truly be present…
body:
Every Tuesday afternoon, I sit in a room full of people whose names I now know by heart.

What started as a programme became a reminder of what it means to truly be present with another person. No agenda. No outcome targets. Just tea, art materials, and the shared humanity of showing up.

The Memory Café at Holborn Community Association isn't what most people picture when they think of arts programming. There are no galleries, no formal exhibitions, no finished masterpieces pinned to walls. What there is: conversation, laughter, the occasional moment of quiet concentration, and the particular kind of warmth that comes when someone feels genuinely seen.

I've learned more about listening in that room than anywhere else.
